title: Open Function
explanation: 'w' opens for writing (overwrites existing content). 'r' is read, 'a' is append, 'x' is exclusive creation.

title: With Statement
text: What is the purpose of using 'with open() as f:'?

options.text: Auto-closes the file after the block
Opens the file in binary mode

explanation: The 'with' statement (context manager) automatically calls f.close() when the block exits, even if an exception occurs. This prevents resource leaks.

title: Read Methods

options.text: A list of strings, one per line
A single string of the entire file

explanation: f.readlines() reads all lines and returns them as a list of strings (each with a trailing newline). f.read() returns a single string. Iterating over f directly yields lines one by one (memory efficient).

title: Binary Mode
text: How do you open a file in binary write mode?
explanation: Binary mode is indicated by appending 'b' to the mode string: 'wb' (write binary), 'rb' (read binary), 'ab' (append binary).

title: File Exists Check
explanation: os.path.exists() is in the os.path module. pathlib.Path.exists() is the modern alternative. The 'os' module itself doesn't have exists() directly.

title: JSON Serialization
text: Which function writes a Python dict to a JSON file?
explanation: json.dump() writes to a file object. json.dumps() returns a JSON string (s = string). The 's' suffix distinguishes string output from file output.

title: Pathlib Usage
text: What is the modern Python way to construct file paths?
explanation: pathlib (Python 3.4+) provides an OOP path interface. The / operator joins path components. os.path.join() is the older approach. Manual string concatenation is fragile across OSes.

title: CSV Reading
text: Which module is standard for CSV file handling?
explanation: The built-in 'csv' module handles CSV files. pandas is third-party. csv.reader() and csv.writer() provide basic CSV I/O without external dependencies.

title: Pickle Module
text: What is the main limitation of pickle serialization?
explanation: Pickle can execute arbitrary code during deserialization, making it unsafe to unpickle untrusted data. JSON is preferred for data interchange. Pickle IS designed for Python objects.

title: Context Manager Protocol
text: Which methods does a class need to implement the context manager protocol (with statement)?
explanation: A context manager implements __enter__ (returns the resource) and __exit__ (cleanup, called when the block exits). This is what enables the 'with' statement.
